quiz.question:
Black moved out of the check. 

In this endgame, Black is up a pawn and the d-pawn looks pretty dangerous. 

How should we defend?

answers.text: We'll keep giving checks with the rook (Ra4+), Black cannot get away from them.

feedback.text: Good job! Black cannot move away from the d4-pawn, since we will just capture it and the resulting position would be an easy draw.

distractors.text: We should first trade the pawns on the queenside with a3. The resulting position will be a draw.

feedback.text: The d-pawn cannot be stopped after the trade, since our king is stuck on the a-file.

distractors.text: We'll go after the b4-pawn with Rh2 and Rc2. The passed a-pawn will provide enough compensation against Black's passed d-pawn.

feedback.text: This plan is too slow! Black's passed d-pawn is too close to the promotion.
